calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-17-2026 Continued To Aug-04-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES: Kenneth Tsang, Esq., specially appearing for the plaintiff. The Order to Show Cause is re-set to August 04, 2026, at 10:30am in Department 610. Judge: Rochelle East; Clerk Z. Dizon; Proceedings not reported. (610)

Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Enter Judgment Under Stipulation
Off Calendar Apr-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.'s unopposed Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Enter Judgment Under Stipulation is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing and consistent with the parties' agreement and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, judgment shall be entered as such: Plaintiff JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A. shall recover $7,641.23 in principal and $0 in costs, for a total judgment of $7,641.23. This is the judgment authorized by the settlement agreement and related events.

Plaintiff never filed a request for dismissal, so no dismissal can be set aside.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order containing the above text verbatim.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed judgment conforming to the requirements in the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court, ideally on the appropriate Judicial Council form.

No proposed order nor proposed judgment received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and a proposed judgment. Plaintiff must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
